page: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, which translates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 480 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

For local storage, the instance is equipped with 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the hardware demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, achieving 7652.04 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 362.34 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-core processing capabilities are strong, as evidenced by high scores in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench compilation and ray tracing tests.

The resource allocation of the c8id.24xlarge makes it highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads that require rapid local storage access without the overhead of network-attached storage. The high density of compute cores relative to memory, combined with top-tier database operations and Redis SET performance, makes this instance suitable for high-throughput database hosting, software compilation, and large-scale web serving. It is also capable of handling medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing efficiently due to its hardware-accelerated instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores at 3.9 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture incorporates advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which drive top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression. With a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps, this instance is designed for high-throughput workloads. It is highly suited for software compilation, database hosting, static web serving, and prompt processing for large language models. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, offering high resource density for demanding enterprise applications. This hardware profile provides strong multi-core capabilities for parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized platform featuring rapid DDR5 memory and extensive local NVMe SSD storage for intensive database and processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 480 MB L3 cache
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET, and multi-threaded decompression
Optimized for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and database workloads
